memory.summary: I have a free afternoon before the Thursday deadline, and I can choose to relax or quickly finish an easy chore.
The easy chore is nearly done, taking only half an hour and leaving me with 5.7 energy despite a sleep debt.
The easy chore is done, but the main project remains untouched as the afternoon slips away.
I made progress on the easy chore, but the main project is still untouched as time passes.
The easy chore is finally complete, but I still face a significant sleep debt and high stress while the main project remains untouched.
I spent two hours on the easy chore, but the main project remains untouched despite my high stress and sleep debt.
The easy chore is nearly done, but my high stress and sleep debt remain while the main project is still untouched.
The easy chore deadline passed unfinished at 52% progress, confirming my inability to complete it despite recent effort.
I worked briefly on the term project, but my exhaustion is extreme. I now realize delaying the future project will compound stress later.
The chore is finally complete, yet my energy remains at zero and stress is high, confirming that finishing small tasks does not alleviate my exhaustion.
Sleeping only two hours left me with critically low energy and high stress, confirming that my recent attempts to finish tasks have failed to restore my capacity.
Completing the chore increased my progress slightly, but with high stress and low energy, I realize small wins alone cannot fix my current exhaustion.
